content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a game consisting of NNN rounds. In each round he rolls a regular six faced dice.

He starts with an initial score of 000. Let the number rolled by him in a particular round be XXX.

If this is not the first round and the previous number rolled was 111, Chef will add 2⋅X2 \cdot X2⋅X to his score, otherwise he will add XXX to his score.

For example, if the game has 666 rounds and the rolls are [2,1,6,1,1,4][2, 1, 6, 1, 1, 4][2,1,6,1,1,4] in the given order, his score will be 2+1+(2⋅6)+1+(2⋅1)+(2⋅4)=262 + 1 + (2 \cdot 6) + 1 + (2 \cdot 1) + (2 \cdot 4) = 262+1+(2⋅6)+1+(2⋅1)+(2⋅4)=26.

Find the **maximum possible score** he can obtain after NNN rounds.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer NNN the number of rounds.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum score that Chef can get.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef can roll [1,6][1, 6][1,6] and his score will be 1+(2⋅6)=131 + (2 \cdot 6) = 131+(2⋅6)=13.
Test case 222: Chef can roll [1,6,1,6][1, 6, 1, 6][1,6,1,6] and his score will be 1+(2⋅6)+1+(2⋅6)=261 + (2 \cdot 6) + 1 + (2 \cdot 6) =261+(2⋅6)+1+(2⋅6)=26.
